mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth accelerates beyond a 2.5 percent annualized quarterly pace, corporate revenue expectations upgrade, driving equity risk premiums lower and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next trading week.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate exceeds 2.5 percent annualized
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of instances where quarterly GDP growth exceeds the 2.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: The joint movement of rising nonfarm payrolls and a falling unemployment rate signals a tightening labor market that boosts household income and consumption demand, which lifts equity valuations over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns when both series move in the specified direction, yielding a directional accuracy below 52 percent over a 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation, an increase in the industrial production index indicates that firms are successfully passing higher input costs to consumers without sacrificing volume, which supports profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when industrial production rises during months where CPI year-over-year inflation exceeds 3.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in housing starts signals rising consumer confidence and credit availability, which drives broad-based equity outperformance over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if housing starts rise for three consecutive months while the S&P 500 delivers negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of those instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 0.5 percent, the market prices in a normalization of monetary policy that compresses equity multiples, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward analysis shows that 20-day forward returns remain positive or statistically flat in more than 50 percent of instances following the 0.5 percent rate threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion characterized by a unemployment rate below 5.0 percent, accelerating payroll growth signals peak labor demand that causes corporate margin compression and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if payroll growth accelerates while the unemployment rate remains below 5.0 percent, yet the S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of such observations.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer spending strength that lifts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns when both retail sales and housing starts increase month-over-month, yielding a directional accuracy below 50 percent over a 3-year sample.
